heading: Safety management system
label: 3203 Safety management system

children.text: a safety and environmental protection policy;
instructions and procedures to ensure safe operation of those vessels and protection of the environment in compliance with international and United States law;
defined levels of authority and lines of communications between, and among, personnel on shore and on the vessel;
procedures for reporting accidents and nonconformities with this chapter;

children.label: A
children.text: prevention;

children.label: B
children.text: bystander intervention;

children.label: C
children.text: reporting;

children.label: D
children.text: response; and

children.label: E
children.text: investigation;
the list required under section 3106(a)(2) and the log book required under section 3106(a)(3);
procedures for preparing for and responding to emergency situations; and
procedures for internal audits and management reviews of the system.

children.heading: .—Procedures and Training Requirements
children.label: b .—Procedures and Training Requirements
children.text: In prescribing regulations for the procedures and training requirements described in subsection (a)(5), such procedures and requirements shall be consistent with the requirements to report sexual harassment or sexual assault under section 10104.

children.heading: .—Suspension
children.label: A .—Suspension
children.text: During an audit of a safety management system of a vessel required under section 10104(e), the Secretary may suspend the Safety Management Certificate issued for the vessel under section 3205 and issue a separate Safety Management Certificate for the vessel to be in effect for a 3-month period beginning on the date of the issuance of such separate certificate.

children.label: i
children.text: that the holder of the Safety Management Certificate knowingly, or repeatedly, failed to comply with section 10104; or

children.label: ii
children.text: other failure of the safety management system resulted in the failure to comply with such section.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Following an audit of the safety management system of a vessel required under section 10104(e), the Secretary may audit the safety management system of the responsible person for the vessel.

children.heading: .—Suspension
children.label: B .—Suspension
children.text: During an audit under subparagraph (A), the Secretary may suspend the Document of Compliance issued to the responsible person under section 3205 and issue a separate Document of Compliance to such person to be in effect for a 3-month period beginning on the date of the issuance of such separate document.

children.label: i
children.text: that the holder of the Document of Compliance knowingly, or repeatedly, failed to comply with section 10104; or

children.label: ii
children.text: that other failure of the safety management system resulted in the failure to comply with such section.

children.heading: .—Compliance With Code
children.label: d .—Compliance With Code
children.text: Regulations prescribed under this section shall be consistent with the International Safety Management Code with respect to vessels to which this chapter applies under .section 3202(a) of this title
the characteristics, methods of operation, and nature of the service of these vessels; and
with respect to vessels that are ferries, the sizes of the ferry systems within which the vessels operate.
